docs.title: GRAG-System
docs.text:
Production-grade Graph Retrieval-Augmented Generation Framework

GRAG-System is an open-source framework designed to enable graph-aware retrieval, multi-hop reasoning, and reinforcement-learning-driven retrieval optimization for modern AI applications.

docs.title: Why GRAG-System?
docs.text:
Traditional Retrieval-Augmented Generation systems rely primarily on vector similarity search. While effective for semantic retrieval, they often struggle with:

  • Relationship-aware reasoning
  • Multi-hop question answering
  • Explainability
  • User-adaptive retrieval strategies
  • Dynamic knowledge structures

GRAG-System addresses these limitations by introducing a graph-centric retrieval architecture.

docs.title: Graph Retrieval
docs.text: Retrieve information through explicit relationships between entities.

docs.title: Multi-hop Reasoning
docs.text: Traverse knowledge graphs across multiple edges to answer complex questions.

docs.title: Query Understanding
docs.text: Analyze user intent, constraints, and entities before retrieval.

docs.title: Reinforcement Learning Feedback
docs.text: Continuously improve retrieval policies based on user feedback.

docs.title: Examples
docs.text: This section demonstrates common usage patterns for GRAG-System.

docs.title: Basic Query
docs.text:
The simplest way to interact with GRAG-System.

from grag import GraphRAG

rag = GraphRAG()

response = rag.query(
    "What is Graph Retrieval Augmented Generation?"
)

print(response)

docs.title: Multi-Hop Reasoning
docs.text:
Traverse relationships between entities.

response = rag.query(

"""
Find researchers working on Graph RAG
who later joined NVIDIA.
"""

)

print(response)

GRAG attempts to perform:

Entity Extraction

↓

Relationship Discovery

↓

Multi-hop Traversal

↓

Evidence Aggregation

↓

Answer Generation

docs.title: Query Understanding
docs.text:
Analyze query structure before retrieval.

from grag.retrieval.query_understanding import QueryParser


parser = QueryParser()

result = parser.parse(

"Find papers written by DeepMind researchers on Graph Neural Networks"

)

print(result)

Example output

{
 "entities":[
      "DeepMind",
      "Graph Neural Networks"
 ],

 "intent":"search",

 "constraints":[],


 "reasoning_depth":2

}

docs.title: Reward Learning
docs.text:
Update retrieval preferences using feedback.

from grag.rl.reward_engine import RewardEngine


reward = RewardEngine()


reward.update(

query="What is RAG?",


response="...",


reward=1.0

)

Negative feedback

reward.update(

query=q,


response=r,


reward=-1

)

docs.title: Knowledge Graph Construction
docs.text:
Build graphs from relationships.

import networkx as nx


graph = nx.Graph()



graph.add_edge(

"GraphRAG",

"NVIDIA"

)


graph.add_edge(

"NVIDIA",

"Research"

)

docs.title: Hybrid Retrieval
docs.text:
Combine semantic retrieval with graph traversal.

results = rag.retrieve(


query="Graph Transformers",


strategy="hybrid"

)

Supported strategies

vector


graph


hybrid

docs.title: OpenAI Integration
docs.text:
from openai import OpenAI


client = OpenAI()



response = rag.query(


question="Explain Graph Attention Networks",



llm=client

)

docs.title: Batch Processing
docs.text:
Evaluate multiple queries.

queries=[

"What is GraphRAG?",


"Explain RLHF",


"Describe Neo4j"

]



for q in queries:


    print(


        rag.query(q)

    )

docs.title: Local Models
docs.text:
Example with Ollama.

response = rag.query(


question="What is GraphRAG?",



model="mistral"

)

docs.title: End-to-End Workflow
docs.text:
from grag import GraphRAG


rag = GraphRAG()



question = """


Find Graph RAG researchers who later joined NVIDIA.

"""



answer = rag.query(question)



print(answer)



reward_engine.update(


query=question,


response=answer,


reward=1.0

)

Pipeline

User Query
↓

Query Understanding
↓

Knowledge Graph
↓

Multi-hop Retrieval
↓

Context Builder
↓

LLM
↓

Answer
↓

User Feedback
↓

Reward Engine

docs.title: Is this production ready?
docs.text:
Core functionality is stable.

Advanced graph features are under active development.

docs.title: Development Setup
docs.text:
Clone the repository.

git clone https://github.com/bobby-nandigam/grag_system.git

cd grag_system

Create environment.

python -m venv .venv

source .venv/bin/activate

Install package.

pip install -e ".[dev]"
